name: Shooting Stars
summary: A background component that generates random shooting stars with customizable speed, color, and spawn timing.
detailedDescription: Shooting Stars creates an animated background effect with streaks of light that shoot across the screen from random edge positions. Each shooting star spawns at a random point along one of the four screen edges and travels diagonally across the viewport at a configurable speed. The stars use SVG rendering with customizable width, height, and colors for both the star body and trail. The component uses requestAnimationFrame for smooth animation, updating star positions based on their angle and speed values. Stars scale up as they travel, creating a sense of depth and perspective. When stars move off-screen, they are removed and new ones spawn after random delays within configurable min/max ranges. The shooting star angles are determined by their spawn edge (45° from top, 135° from right, 225° from bottom, 315° from left). This creates a dynamic, ever-changing background that adds motion and visual interest without overwhelming foreground content. Ideal for hero sections, landing pages, or any context where a subtle cosmic atmosphere enhances the design.
purpose: Create dynamic animated background with moving light streaks
Add subtle motion and depth to page backgrounds
behavior: Spawns shooting stars from random edge positions
Animates stars diagonally across viewport using requestAnimationFrame
Scales stars proportionally to distance traveled
Removes stars when they exit screen bounds
Randomizes spawn timing between min/max delay values
visualCharacteristics: Diagonal light streaks crossing the screen
Stars grow larger as they travel (perspective effect)
Random spawn points from all four edges
recommendations: Best for premium landing page sections and hero compositions.
Use as a supporting atmospheric layer rather than the primary layout structure.
